perturbation.original_text: Recipients as indicated on the attached signature sheet (hereinafter "Recipients" collectively or “Recipient” individually).
perturbation.changed_text: Recipients as indicated on the attached signature sheet (hereinafter "Participants" collectively or “Participant” individually).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Recipients' to 'Participants' introduces ambiguity. Throughout the agreement, the term 'Recipient' defines the obligations. Now, it's unclear if the obligations apply to 'Participants,' creating a loophole. This directly contradicts the enforcement of clauses such as those in Section 1 that states: Recipients (and their respective partners, suppliers, licensors, licencees, agents, contractors and affiliates, who are hereby authorized to have Confidential Information disclosed to them hereunder only so long as they are in receipt of said Confidential Information under terms of confidentiality comparable to those set forth herein) hereby agree to hold in confidence any and all Confidential Information disclosed during the Up-Start Competition, either verbally or in writing. If 'Participants' and 'Recipients' are not unequivocally the same, Participants may not be bound by the confidentiality clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipients (and their respective partners, suppliers, licensors, licencees, agents, contractors and affiliates, who are hereby authorized to have Confidential Information disclosed to them hereunder only so long as they are in receipt of said Confidential Information under terms of confidentiality comparable to those set forth herein) hereby agree to hold in confidence any and all Confidential Information disclosed during the Up-Start Competition, either verbally or in writing. Such obligation of confidentiality shall not apply to Confidential Information: (a) which, at the time of disclosure, is in the public domain or thereafter becomes part of the public domain by publication or otherwise, other than by breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; (b) which was in the Recipient's possession at the time of disclosure and was not acquired, directly or indirectly, from the disclosing party; (c) which the Recipient receives from a third party having a lawful right to disclose the same; (d) in respect of which it can be established, by competent contemporaneous written evidence, that the same was developed by the Recipient independently of such disclosure; or (e) which is required to be disclosed to a third party pursuant to any applicable law or decision of any court or tribunal of competent jurisdiction, provided that (i) prior 2 written notice, in light of the circumstances of such disclosure, is provided by the party required to make the disclosure to the third party to other party hereto; (ii) the party required to make the disclosure to the third party shall not disclose or release more Confidential Information than is required by law; and (iii) the party required to make disclosure to the third party cooperates with the other party hereto in any attempts it may make to obtain a protective order or other appropriate assurance that confidential treatment will be afforded to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines 'Recipients' and their obligations, particularly regarding confidential information. The changed text replaces 'Recipients' with 'Participants,' creating ambiguity as to whether 'Participants' are also bound by the same obligations. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly states the obligations of 'Recipients' to maintain confidentiality. If 'Participants' are not the same as 'Recipients,' then the confidentiality clause may not apply to them, creating a significant loophole and inconsistency in the agreement, aligning with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction perturbation.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, MaRS will be hosting a series of confidential presentations being made by entrants into a business pitch competition (the “Up-Start Competition”); and WHEREAS, entrants into the Up-Start Competition are each in possession of certain confidential information that will be disclosed during their presentation in the competition and through questions thereafter (hereinafter "Confidential Information");
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, MaRS will be hosting a series of confidential presentations being made by entrants into a business pitch competition (the “Innovation Showcase”); and WHEREAS, entrants into the Up-Start Competition are each in possession of certain confidential information that will be disclosed during their presentation in the competition and through questions thereafter (hereinafter "Confidential Information");
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Up-Start Competition" to "Innovation Showcase" in the first 'WHEREAS' clause creates confusion. The agreement refers to 'Up-Start Competition' throughout the rest of the document. This contradicts the definition of the event, making the scope of the agreement unclear. This means that anywhere the document is referencing the Up-Start Competition, there's a contradiction since it's stating the Innovation Showcase is happening, but then referring to the Up-Start Competition
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipients (and their respective partners, suppliers, licensors, licencees, agents, contractors and affiliates, who are hereby authorized to have Confidential Information disclosed to them hereunder only so long as they are in receipt of said Confidential Information under terms of confidentiality comparable to those set forth herein) hereby agree to hold in confidence any and all Confidential Information disclosed during the Up-Start Competition, either verbally or in writing. Such obligation of confidentiality shall not apply to Confidential Information: (a) which, at the time of disclosure, is in the public domain or thereafter becomes part of the public domain by publication or otherwise, other than by breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; (b) which was in the Recipient's possession at the time of disclosure and was not acquired, directly or indirectly, from the disclosing party; (c) which the Recipient receives from a third party having a lawful right to disclose the same; (d) in respect of which it can be established, by competent contemporaneous written evidence, that the same was developed by the Recipient independently of such disclosure; or (e) which is required to be disclosed to a third party pursuant to any applicable law or decision of any court or tribunal of competent jurisdiction, provided that (i) prior 2 written notice, in light of the circumstances of such disclosure, is provided by the party required to make the disclosure to the third party to other party hereto; (ii) the party required to make the disclosure to the third party shall not disclose or release more Confidential Information than is required by law; and (iii) the party required to make disclosure to the third party cooperates with the other party hereto in any attempts it may make to obtain a protective order or other appropriate assurance that confidential treatment will be afforded to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the "Up-Start Competition" and the changed text replaces this with "Innovation Showcase" in the first 'WHEREAS' clause. The contradicted text refers to "Up-Start Competition" explicitly. Because the changed text redefines the name of the competition, it creates an in-text contradiction and misaligned terminology, as specified in the definitions, making the contract's scope unclear since the contradicted text refers to the original name, and the changed text has changed the name. This is a strong contradiction that renders the agreement's scope ambiguous and causes uncertainty about what event the confidentiality agreement applies to. It also directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted text, which refers to the "Up-Start Competition."

perturbation.original_text: Recipients (and their respective partners, suppliers, licensors, licencees, agents, contractors and affiliates, who are hereby authorized to have Confidential Information disclosed to them hereunder only so long as they are in receipt of said Confidential Information under terms of confidentiality comparable to those set forth herein) hereby agree to hold in confidence any and all Confidential Information disclosed during the Up-Start Competition, either verbally or in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipients (and their respective partners, suppliers, licensors, licencees, agents, contractors and affiliates, who are hereby authorized to have Confidential Information disclosed to them hereunder only so long as they are in receipt of said Proprietary Information under terms of confidentiality comparable to those set forth herein) hereby agree to hold in confidence any and all Confidential Information disclosed during the Up-Start Competition, either verbally or in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Information' in the parenthetical clause introduces ambiguity. While the main obligation still refers to 'Confidential Information', the parenthetical clause now suggests that the authorized parties are receiving 'Proprietary Information'. This creates a conflict in interpretation, as it's unclear what information the affiliates are authorized to receive and what the agreement covers. This is problematic because 'Proprietary Information' isn't defined anywhere. Making Section 1's, confidentiality agreement ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipients (and their respective partners, suppliers, licensors, licencees, agents, contractors and affiliates, who are hereby authorized to have Confidential Information disclosed to them hereunder only so long as they are in receipt of said Proprietary Information under terms of confidentiality comparable to those set forth herein) hereby agree to hold in confidence any and all Confidential Information disclosed during the Up-Start Competition, either verbally or in writing. Such obligation of confidentiality shall not apply to Confidential Information: (a) which, at the time of disclosure, is in the public domain or thereafter becomes part of the public domain by publication or otherwise, other than by breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; (b) which was in the Recipient's possession at the time of disclosure and was not acquired, directly or indirectly, from the disclosing party; (c) which the Recipient receives from a third party having a lawful right to disclose the same; (d) in respect of which it can be established, by competent contemporaneous written evidence, that the same was developed by the Recipient independently of such disclosure; or (e) which is required to be disclosed to a third party pursuant to any applicable law or decision of any court or tribunal of competent jurisdiction, provided that (i) prior 2 written notice, in light of the circumstances of such disclosure, is provided by the party required to make the disclosure to the third party to other party hereto; (ii) the party required to make the disclosure to the third party shall not disclose or release more Confidential Information than is required by law; and (iii) the party required to make disclosure to the third party cooperates with the other party hereto in any attempts it may make to obtain a protective order or other appropriate assurance that confidential treatment will be afforded to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Information' in a parenthetical clause, while the main obligation still refers to 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text then discusses exceptions to 'Confidential Information'. Because the parenthetical clause now refers to 'Proprietary Information', which isn't defined, it creates ambiguity and contradiction regarding what information the affiliates are authorized to receive and whether the exceptions in the contradicted text apply to both 'Confidential Information' and the undefined 'Proprietary Information'. Thus, the Changed text introduces ambiguity where certainty is expected.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information disclosed hereunder for any commercial or other purpose other than the purpose described in the third recital to this Agreement, without first entering into a written agreement between the parties covering such other use.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Data disclosed hereunder for any commercial or other purpose other than the purpose described in the third recital to this Agreement, without first entering into a written agreement between the parties covering such other use.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' creates ambiguity about what is being protected. 'Confidential Data' is not defined, so it's unclear whether it encompasses the same information as 'Confidential Information'. This will cause uncertainty about what actions are prohibited by the agreement. In section 2, it changes what can or cannot be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Data disclosed hereunder for any commercial or other purpose other than the purpose described in the third recital to this Agreement, without first entering into a written agreement between the parties covering such other use. Upon receiving a written request from the disclosing party, the receiving party shall cease to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it and shall promptly thereafter return to the disclosing party all written or otherwise documented Confidential Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement, except that one copy of all such Confidential Information may be kept for archival purposes and for the purpose of defending against any lawsuits brought by the disclosing party against the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' creates a contradiction. The explanation states that this introduces ambiguity because 'Confidential Data' is not defined, making it unclear if it encompasses the same information as 'Confidential Information.' The contradicted text uses the term 'Confidential Data,' implying it has a specific meaning within the agreement. If the meaning of 'Confidential Data' is now uncertain due to the change, it directly conflicts with the contradicted text where it's used as if its meaning is clear and consistent throughout the document. Since Misaligned Terminology leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, this change causes such a contradiction, and therefore it is a "YES".

perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges that any violation of any of the provisions hereof by such party may result in immediate and irreparable damage to other party and agrees that in the event of such violation the other party shall, in addition to any other right, relief or remedy at law, be entitled to any equitable relief that any court of competent jurisdiction may deem just and proper.
perturbation.changed_text: Participant acknowledges that any violation of any of the provisions hereof by such party may result in immediate and irreparable damage to other party and agrees that in the event of such violation the other party shall, in addition to any other right, relief or remedy at law, be entitled to any equitable relief that any court of competent jurisdiction may deem just and proper.
perturbation.explanation: Referring to 'Participant' rather than 'Recipient' introduces ambiguity regarding who is bound by the provisions, creating a contradiction and undermining the contract's enforceability. Section 3 makes the liability ambiguous by changing the term of the subject.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Participant acknowledges that any violation of any of the provisions hereof by such party may result in immediate and irreparable damage to other party and agrees that in the event of such violation the other party shall, in addition to any other right, relief or remedy at law, be entitled to any equitable relief that any court of competent jurisdiction may deem just and proper.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Recipient', while the changed text refers to 'Participant'. The contradicted text also refers to 'Participant'. The explanation states that this change introduces ambiguity regarding who is bound by the provisions, creating a contradiction and undermining the contract's enforceability. Since both the changed text and contradicted text now use 'Participant' instead of the original 'Recipient', it causes a misalignment and ambiguity that leads to a strong contradiction as defined by the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to use due care, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care, to keep Confidential Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement in a safe and secure place that is at least as safe and secure as the place where that party keeps other confidential information that it considers to be valuable and proprietary to itself.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees to use due care, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care, to keep Sensitive Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement in a safe and secure place that is at least as safe and secure as the place where that party keeps other confidential information that it considers to be valuable and proprietary to itself.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' creates a contradiction because the agreement is about the former, not the latter. 'Sensitive Information' isn't defined, so it's unclear what standard of care applies to the actual 'Confidential Information'. This makes Section 4 ambiguous. Due to the original texts refference to confidentiality of one element, but here says Sensitive Information, creates and in-text contradition, when it should refer to Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees to use due care, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care, to keep Sensitive Information disclosed to it pursuant to this Agreement in a safe and secure place that is at least as safe and secure as the place where that party keeps other confidential information that it considers to be valuable and proprietary to itself.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' in the Changed Text introduces an in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text also refers to 'Sensitive Information' but the original agreement, as shown in the Original Text, focuses on 'Confidential Information'. Since 'Sensitive Information' is not defined within the agreement, applying the standards for 'Confidential Information' to it creates ambiguity, leading to a conflict and uncertainty about the obligation regarding confidential data. The Perturbation Explanation also states this, making the changed text and contradicted text contradict each other since they reference an undefined 'Sensitive Information' when the core of the agreement is 'Confidential Information'.
